patches.rationale: V6 restructure made Section 2.2 be Tidal Pattern (unrelated). The intended targets are Section 2.3 (Gravity at the Rim: 90% Drop) and Section 2.8 (Gravity Gradient with Latitude). Fix both anchors.

patches.find: Dome predicts 90% gravity drop at rim (H=837 km vs 8,537 at pole); actual variation is 0.53%
patches.replace: Dome's own H(r) implies 90% gravity drop at the rim; observed variation is 0.53% (170× error)
patches.rationale: Current finding is parse-ambiguous: 'H=837 km vs 8,537 at pole' looks like a gravity figure (km units make no sense for g). New wording makes clear H is column height and the 90% drop is derived. Also adds the 170x ratio for clarity.

patches.find: line reading “WGS84 standard.” The dome has two incompatible gravity stories: (1) the H(
patches.replace: line reading “WGS84 standard” (monitor.py, gravity-anomaly domain entry, V51.1). The dome has two incompatible gravity stories: (1) the H(
patches.rationale: Monitor.py citation was unanchored. A defender could demand 'show me the line.' Adding the file, domain, and version anchors the claim so it's verifiable.

patches.find: Under square-root scaling: √(837/8,537) ≈ 0.31, a 69% drop. To reconcile
patches.replace: Under square-root scaling: √(837/8,537) ≈ 0.31, a 69% drop. These two examples bracket the range of plausible aetheric-pressure scalings; even an extreme g ∝ H<sup>1/5</sup> coupling still gives a 38% drop — two orders of magnitude larger than the observed 0.53%. To reconcile
patches.rationale: The 'any monotonic' claim is asserted but the two examples (linear + sqrt) could look like cherry-picking. Adding a third extreme case (H^1/5 = 38%) ties the examples to the broader 'any monotonic mapping fails' argument.
